channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Lawsuit claims Hallmark exec wanted to replace 'old talent' with younger stars
item.description: An age discrimination lawsuit filed against Hallmark claims a network executive wanted to replace seasoned actors with "fresh new faces" and instructed a casting director not to cast "old people.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors seek resentencing for Erik and Lyle Menendez in 1989 killings of their parents
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors recommended Thursday Erik and Lyle Menendez be resentenced for the 1989 killings of their parents in the family’s Beverly Hills home, providing the brothers with a chance at freedom after 34 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, JAIMIE DING and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats invest $5 million to try to defeat Republican Ted Cruz in Texas Senate race
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats backing Rep. Colin Allred's bid to defeat Texas Sen. Ted Cruz are investing $5 million in an ad campaign focused on abortion, making a late-campaign play for what's seen as a safely Republican seat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles vegan restaurant named best in the nation by Yelp
item.description: Perhaps no city in the U.S. has the abundance and demand for vegan cuisine like Los Angeles. So it's probably not surprising to see that the top ranked vegan restaurant in the entire country can be found in the L.A. area.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man killed dog because power wasn't restored after Milton, sheriff says
item.description: "This individual is p---ed off at FP&L, so he shoots and kills his dog," Brevard County Sheriff Wayne Ivey said. "Makes total sense, right? Absolutely not.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners accused of stashing dead bodies plead guilty to federal fraud charges
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado funeral home owners accused of cheating customers and misspending nearly $900,000 in pandemic relief funds, all while allegedly storing 190 decaying bodies in a building and sending grieving families fake ashes, pleaded guilty Thursday to federal fraud charges centered around
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia men who killed Ahmaud Arbery want a new trial
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — The only Black person who served on the jury that convicted three white men of murdering Ahmaud Arbery was called to the witness stand Thursday as defense attorneys sought to make a case that their clients deserve a new trial. The juror, identified in court only as No.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Army soldier’s death investigated as homicide after body found inside Missouri base dumpster
item.description: The death of an Army soldier at Fort Leonard Wood, Missouri, is being investigated as a homicide after the body of 23-year-old Sgt. Sarah Roque was found in a dumpster on the base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A cub of famous Wyoming grizzly No. 399 has been unseen since his mom's death but odds look good
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — The death of the world’s most famous grizzly bear on a highway in western Wyoming has made an orphan of its cub, but biologists say the youngster’s chances of surviving -- even with a chilly mountain winter coming on -- are good.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CDC updates COVID-19 vaccine recommendations for older adults
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is urging some people to get additional doses of the COVID-19 vaccine for stronger protection against the virus.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: Texas DPS surveillance plane captures Mexican cartel factions during gun battle near border
item.description: Texas authorities captured video footage of a gun battle between Gulf Cartel factions in Mexico, just across the southern border on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: County judge strikes down Ohio abortion ban, citing voter-approved reproductive rights amendment
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The most far-reaching of Ohio’s laws restricting abortion was struck down on Thursday by a county judge who said last year’s voter-approved amendment enshrining reproductive rights renders the so-called heartbeat law unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red-cockaded woodpeckers' recovery in southeast leads to status change from endangered to threatened
item.description: The red-cockaded woodpecker, an iconic bird in southeastern forests, has recovered enough of its population to be downlisted from an endangered species to a threatened one.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Attorneys for the three convicted killers are making a range of arguments for a new trial, from a tainted jury to ineffective counsel for one of the men.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, NewsNation

item.title: USPS mailbox in Arizona set on fire, damaging several ballots
item.description: Phoenix police reported that the suspect said the arson was not politically motivated and that he just wanted to be arrested, according to multiple outlets.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks after setting a nationwide deadline for removal of lead pipes, the Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USPS mailbox in Arizona set on fire, damaging several ballots
item.description: Phoenix police reported that the suspect said the arson was not politically motivated and that he just wanted to be arrested, according to multiple outlets.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former correctional officers get prison for using inmates identities in fraud scheme
item.description: At least $331,282 was deposited into the defendants’ fraudulent bank accounts from at least 11 sources.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man killed dog because power wasn't restored after Milton, sheriff says
item.description: A Florida man was arrested after he allegedly shot and killed his dog because he was mad that his power hadn't been restored after Hurricane Milton, according to the sheriff.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: President Biden to apologize for 150-year Indian boarding school policy
item.description: President Joe Biden says he will formally apologize on for the nation's role in forcing Indigenous children into boarding schools, where for more than 150 years many were physically, emotionally and sexually abused, and more than 950 died.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McDonald's E. coli outbreak: Why only the Quarter Pounder was linked
item.description: McDonald's has voluntarily stopped selling its Quarter Pounder hamburger at restaurants across Colorado and other states after an E. coli outbreak was linked to the menu item.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World on pace for significantly more warming without immediate climate action, report warns
item.description: The world is on a path to get 1.8 degrees Celsius (3.2 Fahrenheit) warmer than it is now, but could trim half a degree of that projected future heating if countries do everything they promise to fight climate change, a United Nations report said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aurora authorities knew about Tren de Aragua problem over a year ago, documents show
item.description: Authorities in Aurora, Colorado, were aware of the threat from Tren de Aragua gang members more than a year ago, a city council member says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McDonald's customer files first lawsuit over E. Coli outbreak
item.description: A McDonald’s customer filed the first lawsuit against the fast food chain on Wednesday in connection with an E. coli outbreak linked to the chain’s Quarter Pounder. Eric Stelly, who filed the suit in Cook County Circuit Court in Illinois, ate food from McDonald's Greeley, Colorado, location on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Georgia officials say they thwarted an attempt to crash a state election website
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia election officials acted quickly earlier this month to thwart an attempt to flood the state's absentee voter portal in an apparent attempt to crash the site, the secretary of state's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC recommends lower age for people to get vaccine against pneumonia
item.description: All adults aged 50 and older should receive a vaccine against bacteria that can cause pneumonia and meningitis, federal health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Georgia's Gullah-Geechee community seeks path forward after deadly dock collapse
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The partial collapse of a ferry dock that claimed the lives of seven people attending a cultural festival has left a historic Black community in Georgia struggling with how to move forward.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Travis and Gregory McMichael armed themselves with guns and jumped in a pickup truck to chase Arbery, a 25-year-old Black man, after they saw him run past their house on Feb. 23, 2020.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana governor forces New Orleans homeless encampment to move ahead of Taylor Swift shows
item.description: Ahead of Taylor Swift shows in New Orleans this weekend, Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry authorized the relocation of a homeless encampment from the popular tourist area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boots for dogs become 'Croctober' smash; sell out fast
item.description: Footwear company Crocs developed a special edition boot for dogs in celebration of "Croctober" and they were a big hit. The shoe brand releases a fan-inspired product each October and this year it was all about man's best friend, KTLA sister station WKRG reported earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: Colleges left helpless as students rule out schools due to state politics
item.description: Colleges and universities find themselves increasingly trapped with the politics of the state where they reside.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Arizona teen arrested on terrorism charges in alleged plot against Phoenix Pride Festival
item.description: Marvin Aneer Jalo, 17, allegedly ordered ingredients to make explosives, which were sent to his home, so he could perhaps bomb a Pride parade, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump assassination attempt: Secret Service knew airspace protection would end with former president onstage
item.description: Additional lapses in public airspace restrictions on the day of former President Trump's July 13 rally continues to raise questions and concerns about security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beloved Grand Teton grizzly bear No. 399 fatally struck by a vehicle in Wyoming
item.description: Grand Teton National Park’s famous grizzly bear No. 399, known by wildlife photographers around the world, died after being struck by a vehicle Tuesday in Wyoming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers convene again to address Hurricane Helene's billions in damages
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina state legislators returning to work Thursday to consider further Hurricane Helene relief have received an estimate of the monetary scope of the catastrophic flooding and what Gov. Roy Cooper wants them to spend soon on recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
